title: Sachis Grand Review of Rebuild of Evangelion
display_title: Sachis Grand Review of Rebuild of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Sachi's Grand Review of Rebuild of Evangelion. (SPOILERS)             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Mar 23, 2010 3:24 am As most of you already know, I don't pay a lot of attention to all the Rebuild speculation. Despite this, Rebuild is a popular topic for discussion on the IRC and among many of my other peers; whether it being Rebuild hate, or Rebuild speculation, it never fails to come up at one point or another. It is because of this that I've decided to give my opinion on Rebuild to the world and how I think Rebuild's reception should be, instead of the whole "It's not as good as NGE, lets hate it!" or "This is doing NGE great justice, it's awesome!", and why we should look at Rebuild as another movie, completely independent from the original series, and never, EVAR, compare it to Neon Genesis Evangelion or anything else on a critical level, but simply for the differences at face value. So, without further adieu, I present to you my essay on Rebuild of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Tue Mar 23, 2010 3:48 am Personally, i'm on the oppsoite end of the spectrum when it comes to opinions about Rebuild. The only way to truly appreciate it is having seen the original. So far, the most interesting thing about it is how it's different compared to the original, not how it stands on it's own. I mean, you yourself state that "The new movies promise the bring an entirely new aspect to the expansive universe of Evangelion". But you can't really get a kick out of that unless you have already experienced the Evaverse, no? Rebuild is playing to the fans. Not like that's a bad thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Mar 23, 2010 3:55 am SaltyJoe wrote: So far, the most interesting thing about it is how it's different compared to the original, not how it stands on it's own. I mean, you yourself state that "The new movies promise the bring an entirely new aspect to the expansive universe of Evangelion". But you can't really get a kick out of that unless you have already experienced the Evaverse, no?

[]: Re: Sachi's Grand Review of Rebuild of Evangelion. (SPOILERS             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Mar 23, 2010 5:34 am Sachi wrote: we should look at Rebuild as another movie, completely independent from the original series, and never, EVAR, compare it to Neon Genesis Evangelion or anything else on a critical level, but simply for the differences at face value.

[]:             Quote Post by Jesterhead » Tue Mar 23, 2010 9:45 am The only reasons I enjoy Rebuild so much is because I appriciate and love the series so much. IMO without the series behind it Rebuild isn't entirely amazing. I would never ever dream about suggesting Rebuild over the series. But I would also never ever dream about not watching Rebuild after understanding the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Tue Mar 23, 2010 9:58 am It's certainly par with any top-tier Japanese movie in the last few years. Comparing Summer Wars to Rebuild 2.0, they match blow to blow in entertainment with action and development (though I can't stand Summer War's director's pacing.)

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Fri Mar 26, 2010 4:49 pm I agree with SaltyJoe, the movies are essentially fanservice, not that that's necessarily a bad thing. And I honestly don't see any new fans being able to fully understand them. Rebuild 2.0 especially seems to bank on the viewer's knowledge of the TV series.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Mar 26, 2010 4:53 pm Lucretius wrote: I agree with SaltyJoe, the movies are essentially fanservice, not that that's necessarily a bad thing. And I honestly don't see any new fans being able to fully understand them. Rebuild 2.0 especially seems to bank on the viewer's knowledge of the TV series.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Fri Mar 26, 2010 4:56 pm In any case, comparing remakes to the original is something film reviwers do all the time. I don't see why it should be off-limits.

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Fri Mar 26, 2010 5:42 pm I don't see how Rebuild 2.0 is requiring of any series knowledge. They meet Asuka, Shinji continues his relationship with Ayanami, they fight Angels, oh shiz it's Eva-Angel and Zeruel, movie ends.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Mar 26, 2010 6:12 pm Lucretius wrote: I honestly don't see any new fans being able to fully understand them. Rebuild 2.0 especially seems to bank on the viewer's knowledge of the TV series.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Fri Mar 26, 2010 6:15 pm Plot twists that seem to be aimed at surprising viewers of the original, strangely truncated scenes that are explained more fully in the original (e.g. the flashback to Misato's past), Kaworu showing up and saying he'll make Shinji happy "this time."

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri Mar 26, 2010 6:15 pm Considering the whole EVA-02 debacle, 2.0 makes MORE sense without NGE TV.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Mar 26, 2010 6:18 pm Lucretius wrote: Plot twists that seem to be aimed at surprising viewers of the original

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Fri Mar 26, 2010 6:49 pm Kaworu showing up and saying he'll make Shinji happy "this time."

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Mar 26, 2010 8:08 pm Lucretius wrote: In any case, comparing remakes to the original is something film reviwers do all the time. I don't see why it should be off-limits.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Mar 27, 2010 12:56 am I think i'm gonna rephrase what i wrote earlier: i think it is possible to enjoy Rebuild without having seen the original, but to get the full experience, you had to have seen the original beforehand. Like i said, one of the most interesting features of Rebuild so far is how it deviates from the original plotline, something that just can not be appreciated without pre-established knowledge. I trust Anno and crew to be good enough to make these changes blossom in the following installments, thus creating a truly new animal with a totally different ending. I believe that once Rebuild is finished, it will be truly appreciable on it's own merits. But as of now , it's just well done fanservice with plot twists. And they are playing with fan expectations. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 27, 2010 1:40 am Joseph the PRPD wrote: Main plot twist that comes to mind is Asuka piloting Unit-03 which would, naturally, really surprise people who have viewed the original series. Anything else is nothing major.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Mar 27, 2010 2:07 am SaltyJoe wrote: IF ANNO DOESN'T SHIP THE PAIRING I SUPPORT, I WILL FEEL INVALIDATED!!!! BECAUSE IF EVA TAUGHT ME ANYTHING, IT'S THAT CARTOONS ARE SRS BIZNESS!!! WAAAH THEY CHANGED MY Asuka, WAAAAH!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Mar 27, 2010 2:46 am Sachi wrote: I never said it was off-limits. There's nothing wrong with comparing Rebuild to the original series. I'm more addressing those that are hating on Rebuild because of the differences between it and the original series, such as the change from Soryu to Shikinami, and the amount of characterization lost between the old and the new.
